adults.option_a: Fourteen sons and three daughters — an unusually large family suggesting exceptional divine blessing and honor
adults.option_b: Eight sons and four daughters — an average family for a temple leader
adults.option_c: Twelve sons and twelve daughters — one for each tribe of Israel
adults.option_d: Seven sons and seven daughters — representing completeness and perfection
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 25:5 — "God gave to Heman fourteen sons and three daughters" — this remarkable family size is explicitly attributed to God's gift and connected to Heman's role as "the king's seer."

adults.question: What phrase connects the temple musical ministry to the prophetic tradition?
adults.option_a: The sons of Asaph prophesied according to the order of the king; Jeduthun prophesied with a harp giving thanks and praising the LORD
adults.option_b: The musicians spoke the oracles of God through their instruments
adults.option_c: Music was a form of priestly intercession equivalent to burnt offerings
adults.option_d: The songs of the Levites were counted as binding prophecy for Israel
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 25:1-3 — Asaph's sons "prophesied according to the order of the king"; Jeduthun's sons "prophesied with a harp, to give thanks and to praise the LORD" — music was inseparably linked to prophetic ministry.

adults.question: What is the first lot that came forth, establishing who led the first musical course?
adults.option_a: Joseph of the sons of Asaph
adults.option_b: Gedaliah of the sons of Jeduthun
adults.option_c: Mattaniah of the sons of Heman
adults.option_d: Zaccur of the sons of Asaph
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 25:9 — "Now the first lot came forth for Asaph to Joseph."

adults.question: How many sons did Jeduthun have who served in the musical ministry?
adults.option_a: Six sons
adults.option_b: Four sons
adults.option_c: Eight sons
adults.option_d: Twelve sons
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 25:3 — "Of Jeduthun: the sons of Jeduthun; Gedaliah, and Zeri, and Jeshaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, six, under the hands of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with a harp."

adults.question: What does the organization of temple musicians into 24 rotating courses reveal about David's theology of worship?
adults.option_a: That worship was to be continuous, structured, and Spirit-led, blending institutional regularity with prophetic spontaneity
adults.option_b: That music was merely ceremonial and had no spiritual significance
adults.option_c: That only the most gifted musicians could enter God's presence through song
adults.option_d: That the number 24 had a mystical meaning related to the hours of the day
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 25:1 — The combination of "separated to the service" (institutional structure) and "prophesy with harps" (Spirit-led expression) shows that David designed temple worship to be both ordered and Spirit-infused.

kids.question: What job did the gatekeepers do at the temple?
kids.option_a: They guarded the gates and doors of the house of the LORD
kids.option_b: They collected offerings from the people
kids.option_c: They led worship with musical instruments
kids.option_d: They served food at the temple feasts
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:12 — "Among these were the divisions of the porters, even among the chief men, having wards one against another, to minister in the house of the LORD."

kids.question: What were kept in the treasuries that the Levites guarded?
kids.option_a: Dedicated things and the spoils won in battles
kids.option_b: The robes and garments of the priests
kids.option_c: The food offerings and grain for the feasts
kids.option_d: The scrolls and books of the law
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:26-27 — "Which Shelomith and his brethren were over all the treasures of the dedicated things, which David the king...had dedicated...out of the spoils won in battles."

kids.question: On how many sides of the temple were there gates that the gatekeepers guarded?
kids.option_a: On every side — south, north, east, and west
kids.option_b: Only on the east and west sides
kids.option_c: Only the main entrance on the south
kids.option_d: On two sides, east and north
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:13-16 — Lots were cast for gates on every side: the east gate, the north gate, the south gate, the west gate with the Shallecheth gate on the causeway.

teens.question: Who was the famous ancestor of Obed-edom whose family provided many gatekeepers?
teens.option_a: Obed-edom himself, whom God had blessed when the ark rested in his house
teens.option_b: Korah the son of Levi whose descendants were forgiven
teens.option_c: Phinehas the son of Eleazar who stopped the plague
teens.option_d: Zeruiah the sister of David whose sons were mighty warriors
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:4-5 — "Moreover the sons of Obed-edom were, Shemaiah the firstborn...for God blessed him." This refers to the same Obed-edom in whose house the ark rested (1 Chronicles 13:14).

teens.question: How many gatekeepers were assigned to the south gate and the storehouse?
teens.option_a: Four toward the south and two at the storehouse
teens.option_b: Six toward the south and four at the storehouse
teens.option_c: Two toward the south and two at the storehouse
teens.option_d: Eight toward the south and four at the storehouse
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:15 — "To Obed-edom southward; and to his sons the house of Asuppim." The divisions were assigned to specific positions around the temple complex.

teens.question: What administrative roles did some Levites serve outside the temple itself?
teens.option_a: Officers and judges over Israel for the outward business, to decide causes and legal matters
teens.option_b: Tax collectors and census-takers for the king
teens.option_c: Military commanders over the royal guard
teens.option_d: Scribes who copied the law for all the cities
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:29 — "Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons were for the outward business over Israel, for officers and judges."

teens.question: What was the name of Obed-edom's firstborn son, and how many able men did Obed-edom's household provide?
teens.option_a: Shemaiah was the firstborn; sixty-two able men came from Obed-edom's household
teens.option_b: Joah was the firstborn; forty able men came from his household
teens.option_c: Zabad was the firstborn; thirty able men came from his household
teens.option_d: Elzabad was the firstborn; twelve able men came from his household
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:4-8 — "Moreover the sons of Obed-edom were, Shemaiah the firstborn...all the sons of Obed-edom were threescore and two."

teens.question: Who had oversight over the treasures of the dedicated things that came from the victories of Samuel, Saul, Abner, and Joab?
teens.option_a: Shelomith and his brethren
teens.option_b: Ahiah and the sons of Jeduthun
teens.option_c: Hosah and his relatives from Merari
teens.option_d: Meshelemiah and the sons of Asaph
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:26-28 — "Which Shelomith and his brethren were over all the treasures of the dedicated things, which David the king...Samuel the seer...Saul...Abner...Joab...had dedicated."

adults.question: What family of Levites from Korah provided the chief gatekeepers according to this chapter?
adults.option_a: Meshelemiah the son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph
adults.option_b: Obed-edom the son of Jeduthun, of the sons of Merari
adults.option_c: Hosah the son of Jediah, of the sons of Merari
adults.option_d: Zechariah the firstborn of Meshelemiah
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:1 — "Concerning the divisions of the porters: Of the Korhites was Meshelemiah the son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph."

adults.question: How were the gate assignments determined among the gatekeepers?
adults.option_a: By lot, according to the house of their fathers, great as well as the small
adults.option_b: By rank of seniority within each Levitical family
adults.option_c: By the king's personal appointment of the most trusted men
adults.option_d: By a competition of skill and strength among the gatekeepers
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:13 — "And they cast lots, as well the small as the great, according to the house of their fathers, for every gate."

adults.question: What specific gate received the lot for Shuppim and Hosah on the west side?
adults.option_a: The gate Shallecheth by the causeway going up
adults.option_b: The gate of the north side
adults.option_c: The gate of the east side
adults.option_d: The Parbar gate on the west
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:16 — "To Shuppim and Hosah the lot came forth westward, with the gate Shallecheth, by the causeway of the going up, ward against ward."

adults.question: How many gatekeepers were assigned daily, and how were the Parbar positions staffed?
adults.option_a: Four each day for the four gates; six at the causeway, four at the Parbar
adults.option_b: Three each day; two at each gate of the compass
adults.option_c: Eight each day; twelve at the Shallecheth gate
adults.option_d: Two each day; twelve total for all positions combined
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:17-18 — "Eastward were six Levites, northward four a day, southward four a day, and toward Asuppim two and two. At Parbar westward, four at the causeway, and two at Parbar."

adults.question: What is said about Zechariah the son of Meshelemiah that distinguishes him among the gatekeepers?
adults.option_a: He was a wise counsellor who was given the lot for the north gate
adults.option_b: He was the strongest man and guarded the east gate alone
adults.option_c: He was the firstborn son and became chief gatekeeper
adults.option_d: He was appointed treasurer because of his honesty
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:14 — "And the lot eastward fell to Shelemiah. Then for Zechariah his son, a wise counsellor, they cast lots; and his lot came out northward."

adults.question: Who was appointed over the treasures of the house of God from the Amramites?
adults.option_a: Shebuel the son of Gershom, the son of Moses
adults.option_b: Phinehas the son of Eleazar who was the ruler
adults.option_c: Ahijah was over the treasures of the dedicated things
adults.option_d: Meshelemiah the son of Kore kept the treasure rooms
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:24 — "And Shebuel the son of Gershom, the son of Moses, was ruler of the treasures."

adults.question: Which territories were mentioned for the Levitical officers who served in the outward business?
adults.option_a: Beyond Jordan westward, in all business of the LORD, and in the service of the king
adults.option_b: Only in Jerusalem and the cities of Judah
adults.option_c: In the northern territories of Ephraim and Manasseh
adults.option_d: In the wilderness regions where the tabernacle had formerly stood
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:30 — "And of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his brethren, men of valour, a thousand and seven hundred, were officers among them of Israel on this side Jordan westward in all the business of the LORD, and in the service of the king."

adults.question: What does the comprehensive organization of gatekeepers, treasurers, and judges reveal about David's vision for the temple as an institution?
adults.option_a: That the temple was to be a complete center of national life — spiritual, administrative, financial, and judicial — not merely a place of sacrifice
adults.option_b: That David wanted to give the Levites maximum employment opportunities
adults.option_c: That the temple treasury was the main source of government revenue
adults.option_d: That gatekeepers were the most important class of temple servants
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 26:29-32 — The extension of Levitical oversight to "outward business," "officers and judges," and territory "beyond Jordan" shows David designed the temple community to serve as the administrative heart of the whole nation.

kids.question: How many military divisions did David organize, one for each month of the year?
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:1 — "Now the children of Israel after their number, to wit, the chief fathers and captains of thousands and hundreds, and their officers that served the king in any matter of the courses, which came in and went out month by month throughout all the months of the year."

kids.question: How many men were in each monthly military division?
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:1-2 — Each course had "four and twenty thousand" as seen in verse 2: "Over the first course for the first month was Jashobeam...and in his course were four and twenty thousand."

kids.question: Who was the prince over the tribe of Judah?
kids.option_a: Elihu, one of the brethren of David
kids.option_d: Hashabiah of the tribe of Levi
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:18 — "Of Judah, Elihu, one of the brethren of David."

teens.question: Who was over the first division for the first month, and what heroic deed was he known for?
teens.option_a: Jashobeam the son of Zabdiel; he slew three hundred in a single encounter
teens.option_b: Dodai the Ahohite; he was one of the thirty mighty men
teens.option_c: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada; he slew two lion-like men of Moab
teens.option_d: Shammoth the Harorite; he defended Israel at Bethlehem
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:2 — "Over the first course for the first month was Jashobeam the son of Zabdiel: and in his course were four and twenty thousand." He is identified with the mighty man of 1 Chronicles 11:11.

teens.question: Who was commander of the third division, and what month did his course serve?
teens.option_a: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, a chief priest, over the third month
teens.option_b: Shammoth the Harorite for the third month
teens.option_c: Asahel the brother of Joab for the third month
teens.option_d: Ikkesh the Tekoite for the third month
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:5 — "The third captain of the host for the third month was Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, a chief priest: and in his course were four and twenty thousand."

teens.question: Why did David not number those under twenty years old, according to this chapter?
teens.option_a: Because the LORD had said he would increase Israel like the stars of heaven
teens.option_b: Because they were exempt from military service by Mosaic law
teens.option_c: Because there were already too many men in the monthly divisions
teens.option_d: Because their fathers paid a ransom for them at birth
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:23 — "But David took not the number of them from twenty years old and under: because the LORD had said he would increase Israel like to the stars of the heavens."

teens.question: Who were the two men over David's herds in Sharon and in the valleys?
teens.option_a: Shitrai the Sharonite was over the flocks in Sharon; Shaphat the son of Adlai over the herds in the valleys
teens.option_b: Jaziz the Hagarite and Obil the Ishmaelite
teens.option_c: Joah the son of Zimmah and Eden the son of Joah
teens.option_d: Bani the Gadite and Mibhar the son of Haggeri
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:29 — "And over the herds that fed in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite: and over the herds that were in the valleys was Shaphat the son of Adlai."

teens.question: Who was Ahithophel, and who replaced him as David's counsellor?
teens.option_a: Ahithophel was the king's counsellor; Jehoiada and Abiathar were after him, and Joab commanded the king's army
teens.option_b: Ahithophel was the royal scribe; Shemaiah replaced him
teens.option_c: Ahithophel was the chief of the guard; Benaiah replaced him
teens.option_d: Ahithophel was commander of the army; Joab succeeded him
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:33-34 — "And Ahithophel was the king's counsellor: and Hushai the Archite was the king's companion: And after Ahithophel was Jehoiada the son of Benaiah, and Abiathar."

adults.question: What is the total size of the standing army implied by twelve monthly divisions of twenty-four thousand each?
adults.option_a: Two hundred eighty-eight thousand men on rotation, with twenty-four thousand available each month
adults.option_b: One hundred forty-four thousand permanently armed soldiers
adults.option_c: Twelve thousand men available to the king at all times
adults.option_d: Four hundred thousand men in total including reserves
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:1-15 — Twelve courses each of 24,000 = 288,000 total on annual rotation; at any given month, 24,000 were in active service while the rest returned to civilian life.

adults.question: Who was over the king's vineyards, and who was over the wine cellars?
adults.option_a: Shimei the Ramathite was over the vineyards; Zabdi the Shiphmite was over the increase of the vineyards for the wine cellars
adults.option_b: Joah of the tribe of Benjamin and Mattaniah the Levite
adults.option_c: Ezri the son of Chelub and Ahijah the Pelonite
adults.option_d: Baal-hanan the Gederite and Joash the storekeeper
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:27 — "And over the vineyards was Shimei the Ramathite: over the increase of the vineyards for the wine cellars was Zabdi the Shiphmite."

adults.question: Who was over the olive trees and the sycamore trees in the lowlands?
adults.option_a: Baal-hanan the Gederite
adults.option_b: Shaphat the son of Adlai
adults.option_c: Obil the Ishmaelite
adults.option_d: Azmaveth the son of Adiel
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:28 — "And over the olive trees and the sycomore trees that were in the low plains was Baal-hanan the Gederite."

adults.question: What was the reason that the wrath of God fell on Israel during David's census, referenced at the end of this chapter?
adults.option_a: The numbers were not finished because wrath fell on Israel, and the numbers from twenty and below were not put in the account
adults.option_b: David failed to consult the Urim and Thummim before ordering the count
adults.option_c: Joab conducted the census without fasting and prayer
adults.option_d: The census violated a specific prohibition in the law of Moses
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:24 — "Joab the son of Zeruiah began to number, but he finished not, because there fell wrath for it against Israel."

adults.question: Who was over the storehouses in the field, in the cities, in the villages, and in the castles?
adults.option_a: Azmaveth the son of Adiel was over the king's treasures; Jonathan the son of Uzziah over the storehouses in the fields
adults.option_b: Obil the Ishmaelite over the camels; Jaziz the Hagarite over the flocks
adults.option_c: Ezri the son of Chelub over the farming laborers
adults.option_d: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada over the king's personal treasury
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:25 — "And over the king's treasures was Azmaveth the son of Adiel: and over the storehouses in the fields, in the cities, and in the villages, and in the castles, was Jehonathan the son of Uzziah."

adults.question: How many tribes of Israel had princes listed in chapter 27, and which tribe was notably absent?
adults.option_a: Eleven tribes were listed with princes; the tribe of Gad was absent from the listed princes
adults.option_b: All twelve tribes had princes equally listed
adults.option_c: Only seven tribes had designated princes
adults.option_d: Ten tribes were listed; Dan and Naphtali were absent
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:16-22 — The tribes listed include Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Aaron, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun, Naphtali, Ephraim, half-Manasseh, Benjamin, Dan; Gad and Asher are not mentioned separately.

adults.question: Who was noted as David's personal advisor who was also called "the king's companion"?
adults.option_a: Hushai the Archite
adults.option_b: Jonathan the son of Shage
adults.option_c: Ira the Ithrite
adults.option_d: Naharai the Berothite
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:33 — "And Ahithophel was the king's counsellor: and Hushai the Archite was the king's companion."

adults.question: What does the comprehensive administrative structure in chapter 27 reveal about the nature of David's monarchy?
adults.option_a: It was a highly organized state where spiritual, military, agricultural, and civil administration were all brought under divinely ordered structure
adults.option_b: It was primarily a military government that secondarily managed civilian affairs
adults.option_c: David's administration was chaotic and this organization was added by later scribes
adults.option_d: The administration was centralized only in Jerusalem with no outreach to the tribes
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 27:1-34 — The chapter covers military (vv. 1-15), tribal leaders (vv. 16-24), agricultural managers (vv. 25-31), and royal advisors (vv. 32-34) — showing a fully integrated national administration undergirded by God's ordering principle.

kids.question: What did David give Solomon for building the temple?
kids.option_a: The pattern of all the temple, its courts, chambers, and treasuries
kids.option_b: A bag of gold and silver coins
kids.option_c: A list of workers to help him build
kids.option_d: The keys to the city of Jerusalem
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:11-12 — "Then David gave to Solomon his son the pattern of the porch, and of the houses thereof...And the pattern of all that he had by the spirit."

kids.question: What did God say about Solomon that made him chosen to build the temple?
kids.option_a: I have chosen him to be my son, and I will be his father
kids.option_b: He is the wisest man in all the earth
kids.option_c: He has never sinned before me
kids.option_d: He is the strongest of all David's sons
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:6 — "And he said unto me, Solomon thy son, he shall build my house and my courts: for I have chosen him to be my son, and I will be his father."

kids.question: What famous words did David tell Solomon to be strong and not to fear?
kids.option_a: Be strong and courageous and do it; fear not, nor be dismayed, for the LORD God will be with thee
kids.option_b: The LORD fights for you, so be silent and trust him
kids.option_c: Do not lean on your own understanding but trust the LORD
kids.option_d: The battle is the LORD's and he will deliver the enemy into your hand
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:20 — "Be strong and of good courage, and do it: fear not, nor be dismayed: for the LORD God, even my God, will be with thee; he will not fail thee, nor forsake thee."

teens.question: Why did God say David could not build the temple himself?
teens.option_a: Because David had shed much blood upon the earth in God's sight
teens.option_b: Because David was too old and weak to oversee the construction
teens.option_c: Because God had chosen Jerusalem only after David's death
teens.option_d: Because the law required a priest to dedicate the temple
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:3 — "But God said unto me, Thou shalt not build an house for my name, because thou hast been a man of war, and hast shed blood."

teens.question: How did David say he received the temple blueprint — by his own wisdom or another way?
teens.option_a: He had it in writing from the hand of the LORD upon him, even all the works of the pattern
teens.option_b: He received it in a vision during the night
teens.option_c: He designed it himself with the help of skilled craftsmen from Tyre
teens.option_d: He copied it from the pattern of the tabernacle given to Moses
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:19 — "All this, said David, the LORD made me understand in writing by his hand upon me, even all the works of this pattern."

teens.question: What warning did David give Solomon about keeping the commandments?
teens.option_a: If thou seek him, he will be found of thee; but if thou forsake him, he will cast thee off forever
teens.option_b: The LORD will bless you if you obey but send enemies if you stray
teens.option_c: Guard the law with your life, for it is more precious than gold
teens.option_d: Do not add to or take away from the commandments of Moses
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:9 — "If thou seek him, he will be found of thee; but if thou forsake him, he will cast thee off for ever."

teens.question: What does David say God knows about people that should motivate Solomon to serve with a perfect heart?
teens.option_a: The LORD searcheth all hearts and understandeth all the imaginations of the thoughts
teens.option_b: The LORD counts the hairs of every man's head and knows all their ways
teens.option_c: God watches from heaven and sees every deed done in secret
teens.option_d: The LORD reads men's lips and knows every prayer before it is spoken
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:9 — "For the LORD searcheth all hearts, and understandeth all the imaginations of the thoughts."

teens.question: What specific details were included in the pattern David gave Solomon for the golden furnishings?
teens.option_a: The pattern for the chariot of the cherubims, the candlesticks, the tables of shewbread, and all the vessels of gold and silver
teens.option_b: Only the measurements of the walls and the height of the pillars
teens.option_c: The names of all the craftsmen who would do the work
teens.option_d: A list of the sacrifices required for the dedication ceremony
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:11-18 — David gave patterns for the porch, houses, treasuries, chambers, parlours, place of the mercy seat, the candlesticks, the tables, the fleshhooks, the chariot of the cherubims.

adults.question: To what assembly did David gather all Israel for this final public charge?
adults.option_a: All the princes of Israel, the princes of the tribes, the captains of the companies, the stewards, the mighty men, and all the valiant men
adults.option_b: Only the seventy elders of Israel in Jerusalem
adults.option_c: The priests, Levites, and prophets only
adults.option_d: The tribal heads and the judges of the land
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:1 — "And David assembled all the princes of Israel, the princes of the tribes, and the captains of the companies that ministered to the king by course, and the captains over the thousands, and captains over the hundreds, and the stewards over all the substance and possession of the king, and of his sons, with the officers, and with the mighty men, and with all the valiant men, unto Jerusalem."

adults.question: What specific theological truth did David declare about God's choice of Judah, Jesse's house, and David personally?
adults.option_a: The LORD chose Judah to be the ruler; out of the house of Judah he chose David's father's house; among the sons of Jesse he liked David to make him king over all Israel
adults.option_b: God chose Israel because they were the smallest of nations
adults.option_c: The LORD chose David because he was more righteous than all his brothers
adults.option_d: God selected Judah by lot and David by the counsel of the prophets
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:4 — "Howbeit the LORD God of Israel chose me before all the house of my father to be king over Israel for ever: for he hath chosen Judah to be the ruler; and of the house of Judah, the house of my father; and among the sons of my father he liked me to make me king over all Israel."

adults.question: What specific weight of gold did David give for the golden candlesticks by pattern?
adults.option_a: By weight for every candlestick, and for the lamps thereof, of gold by weight for every lamp
adults.option_b: One hundred talents of gold per candlestick
adults.option_c: Fifty shekels of gold for the lampstand and twenty for the lamps
adults.option_d: David did not specify weight but only the design
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:15 — "Even the weight for the candlesticks of gold, and for their lamps of gold, by weight for every candlestick, and for the lamps thereof: and for the candlesticks of silver by weight, both for the candlestick, and also for the lamps thereof, according to the use of every candlestick."

adults.question: What Hebrew phrase describes how David received the temple blueprint, and what does it imply about inspiration?
adults.option_a: "The LORD made me understand in writing by his hand upon me" — implying direct divine inscription or revelation of the architectural plan
adults.option_b: David studied the tabernacle dimensions and extrapolated the temple design
adults.option_c: The plans were given through the prophet Nathan in a written scroll
adults.option_d: David hired Phoenician architects and God approved their design
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:19 — "All this, said David, the LORD made me understand in writing by his hand upon me, even all the works of this pattern" — the phrase "by his hand upon me" suggests direct prophetic inspiration.

adults.question: What did David say about Solomon's character in verse 9 as a charge to him about serving God?
adults.option_a: To serve God with a perfect heart and a willing mind
adults.option_b: To pray three times a day and study the law night and day
adults.option_c: To lead Israel in righteousness and not follow the customs of the nations
adults.option_d: To love the LORD with all his heart and build the temple without delay
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:9 — "And thou, Solomon my son, know thou the God of thy father, and serve him with a perfect heart and with a willing mind."

adults.question: What amount of gold did David designate for the altar of incense and the chariot of the cherubims?
adults.option_a: Gold by weight for the altar of incense; six hundred talents of refined gold for the chariot of the cherubims
adults.option_b: One hundred talents for the incense altar; two hundred for the cherubims' chariot
adults.option_c: Fifty shekels for the incense altar; one talent for each cherubim
adults.option_d: David specified design only and left the weight to the craftsmen's discretion
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:17-18 — "Also pure gold for the fleshhooks, and the bowls, and the cups: and for the golden basons he gave gold by weight for every bason...and gold for the pattern of the chariot of the cherubims."

adults.question: What famous promise did David echo from Deuteronomy in his encouragement to Solomon?
adults.option_a: The LORD thy God is with thee; he will not fail thee nor forsake thee
adults.option_b: Be strong and very courageous; do not turn to the right or the left
adults.option_c: I will never leave thee nor forsake thee, saith the LORD of hosts
adults.option_d: He shall give his angels charge over thee to keep thee in all thy ways
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:20 — "He will not fail thee, nor forsake thee" — echoing Deuteronomy 31:6 and 31:8 where Moses used the same words when commissioning Joshua for the conquest.

adults.question: What is the significance of David's public assembly and charge in chapter 28 in terms of dynastic and theological legacy?
adults.option_a: It formally transferred divine mandate, architectural revelation, and covenant responsibility from David to Solomon publicly before all Israel, ensuring institutional continuity of the temple project
adults.option_b: It was primarily a political ceremony to prevent Adonijah from challenging Solomon again
adults.option_c: David wanted to ensure the craftsmen were properly paid before he died
adults.option_d: The assembly was required by Mosaic law before any royal succession
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 28:1-20 — The assembly gathered "all Israel" as witnesses; David revealed the divine origin of the plans, gave the written pattern, and repeated God's covenant promises — making Solomon's task not merely political but covenantally binding before the whole nation.

kids.question: What did David and all the people bring as offerings for the temple?
kids.option_a: Gold, silver, brass, iron, and precious stones willingly given
kids.option_b: Sheep and oxen for burnt offerings
kids.option_c: Bread and wine for the feast of dedication
kids.option_d: Logs of cedar and stone from the quarries
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:6-7 — "Then the chief of the fathers and princes of the tribes of Israel, and the captains of thousands and of hundreds...offered willingly...of gold five thousand talents and ten thousand drams, and of silver ten thousand talents."

kids.question: What famous words did David pray about all the offerings, saying everything belongs to God?
kids.option_a: All things come of thee, and of thine own have we given thee
kids.option_b: We give back to you what you first gave to us
kids.option_c: Blessed be the LORD who provides for all our needs
kids.option_d: All our gold and silver belongs to the house of the LORD
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:14 — "But who am I, and what is my people, that we should be able to offer so willingly after this sort? for all things come of thee, and of thine own have we given thee."

kids.question: What happened to Solomon at the end of this chapter?
kids.option_a: He was anointed king and the people accepted him
kids.option_b: He began building the temple immediately
kids.option_c: He went to Gibeon to offer sacrifices
kids.option_d: He traveled to Egypt to get workers
kids.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:22 — "And they made Solomon the son of David king the second time, and anointed him unto the LORD to be the chief governor, and Zadok to be priest."

teens.question: How much did David give from his own personal treasure for the temple over and above what he had prepared?
teens.option_a: Three thousand talents of gold and seven thousand talents of refined silver
teens.option_b: One hundred thousand talents of gold and a million talents of silver
teens.option_c: Ten thousand talents of gold and twenty thousand of silver
teens.option_d: Five hundred talents of gold and a thousand talents of silver
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:4 — "Even three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir, and seven thousand talents of refined silver, to overlay the walls of the houses withal."

teens.question: What was the people's emotional response when they gave their offerings willingly?
teens.option_a: They rejoiced with great joy because with perfect heart they offered willingly to the LORD
teens.option_b: They wept with gratitude and fell on their faces before God
teens.option_c: They sang praises and danced before the ark
teens.option_d: They were silent in solemn reverence as the offerings were counted
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:9 — "Then the people rejoiced, for that they offered willingly, because with perfect heart they offered willingly to the LORD: and David the king also rejoiced with great joy."

teens.question: What does David's prayer say about human life on earth, using a metaphor of travelers?
teens.option_a: We are strangers before thee and sojourners, as were all our fathers; our days on earth are as a shadow, and there is none abiding
teens.option_b: Man is like a flower of the field that flourishes in the morning and withers at night
teens.option_c: Our life is a vapor that appears for a moment and then passes away
teens.option_d: We are pilgrims and wanderers with no city but the heavenly Jerusalem
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:15 — "For we are strangers before thee, and sojourners, as were all our fathers: our days on the earth are as a shadow, and there is none abiding."

teens.question: Who was anointed along with Solomon at the assembly, and to what office?
teens.option_a: Zadok was anointed to be priest
teens.option_b: Nathan the prophet was anointed as chief counsellor
teens.option_c: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was anointed as army commander
teens.option_d: Joab was anointed as captain of the host
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:22 — "And they made Solomon the son of David king the second time, and anointed him unto the LORD to be the chief governor, and Zadok to be priest."

teens.question: How long did David reign as king over all Israel?
teens.option_a: Forty years — seven years in Hebron and thirty-three years in Jerusalem
teens.option_b: Thirty-three years — all in Jerusalem
teens.option_c: Forty years — all in Jerusalem
teens.option_d: Thirty years — ten in Hebron and twenty in Jerusalem
teens.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:27 — "And the time that he reigned over Israel was forty years; seven years reigned he in Hebron, and thirty and three years reigned he in Jerusalem."

adults.question: What total amount of materials does David say he has prepared for the temple in his opening speech?
adults.option_a: Three million talents of gold, seven million of silver, and countless quantities of brass, iron, wood, stone, marble and precious stones
adults.option_b: A hundred thousand talents of gold and a thousand thousand of silver along with brass, iron, wood, and stone
adults.option_c: Five hundred thousand talents of gold and all the spoils of his wars
adults.option_d: He does not give exact figures but says it was exceedingly magnifical
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:3-5 — David itemizes the gold of Ophir, refined silver, brass, iron, wood, onyx, and precious stones, stating the temple must be "exceeding magnifical" and inviting the leaders to consecrate themselves by giving.

adults.question: How much gold and silver did the leaders of Israel collectively give over and above what David contributed personally?
adults.option_a: Five thousand talents and ten thousand drams of gold; ten thousand talents of silver; eighteen thousand talents of brass; and one hundred thousand talents of iron
adults.option_b: One thousand talents of gold and five thousand of silver from the princes
adults.option_c: Two thousand talents of gold and four thousand of silver total
adults.option_d: The text does not specify the leaders' contribution separately from David's
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:7 — "And gave for the service of the house of God of gold five thousand talents and ten thousand drams, and of silver ten thousand talents, and of brass eighteen thousand talents, and one hundred thousand talents of iron."

adults.question: What are the six attributes or titles David ascribes to God in his prayer of praise?
adults.option_a: Greatness, power, glory, victory, majesty, and the kingdom — all in heaven and earth
adults.option_b: Holiness, righteousness, mercy, faithfulness, wisdom, and love
adults.option_c: Creator, king, judge, redeemer, shepherd, and father
adults.option_d: Almighty, eternal, omniscient, omnipresent, sovereign, and merciful
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:11 — "Thine, O LORD, is the greatness, and the power, and the glory, and the victory, and the majesty: for all that is in the heaven and in the earth is thine; thine is the kingdom, O LORD, and thou art exalted as head above all."

adults.question: What did David ask God to do for Solomon specifically in the prayer?
adults.option_a: Give Solomon a perfect heart to keep God's commandments, testimonies, and statutes, and to build the palace for which David had made provision
adults.option_b: Make Solomon wiser than all the kings of the earth
adults.option_c: Give Solomon long life and victory over all his enemies
adults.option_d: Protect Solomon from the plots of his brothers and the enemies of Israel
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:19 — "And give unto Solomon my son a perfect heart, to keep thy commandments, thy testimonies, and thy statutes, and to do all these things, and to build the palace, for the which I have made provision."

adults.question: What does David say about the source of wealth and honor in his prayer — crediting God or human effort?
adults.option_a: Both riches and honour come of thee, and thou reignest over all; in thine hand is power and might; it is not in any man's power to do it
adults.option_b: Hard work and faithfulness bring blessing while laziness brings poverty
adults.option_c: Those who store up riches in their heart will find God's favor withdrawn
adults.option_d: Wealth comes from God but its keeping depends on man's faithfulness
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:12 — "Both riches and honour come of thee, and thou reignest over all; and in thine hand is power and might; and in thine hand it is to make great, and to give strength unto all."

adults.question: What thousand-offering sacrifice did Solomon and all Israel make after the anointing?
adults.option_a: A thousand bullocks, a thousand rams, a thousand lambs with their drink offerings, and sacrifices in abundance for all Israel
adults.option_b: Seven thousand bullocks and fourteen thousand sheep
adults.option_c: Five hundred burnt offerings and five hundred peace offerings
adults.option_d: One hundred bulls, one hundred rams, and one thousand lambs
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:21 — "And they sacrificed sacrifices unto the LORD, and offered burnt offerings unto the LORD, on the morrow after that day, even a thousand bullocks, a thousand rams, and a thousand lambs, with their drink offerings."

adults.question: Where were the acts of David the king, first and last, written down according to the closing verses?
adults.option_a: In the book of Samuel the seer, in the book of Nathan the prophet, and in the book of Gad the seer
adults.option_b: In the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel
adults.option_c: In the annals of Jerusalem kept by the Levitical scribes
adults.option_d: In the book of the law found in the house of the LORD
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:29 — "Now the acts of David the king, first and last, behold, they are written in the book of Samuel the seer, and in the book of Nathan the prophet, and in the book of Gad the seer."

adults.question: What is the theological climax of 1 Chronicles as expressed in David's final prayer and the conclusion of chapter 29?
adults.option_a: That all human endeavor — kingship, wealth, temple-building — flows from God's sovereign grace, and the proper response is joyful, voluntary worship returned to the God from whom all things come
adults.option_b: That the temple is the greatest achievement of Israel and secures God's favor permanently
adults.option_c: That David's dynasty will never fail because of the covenant made at Hebron
adults.option_d: That human obedience is the foundation of divine blessing and the temple proves Israel's faithfulness
adults.explanation: 1 Chronicles 29:14 — "Of thine own have we given thee" — this phrase, at the center of David's prayer, captures Chronicles' theology: God is the source of all, human worship is returning God's gifts to him, and the temple is the culmination of this divine-human partnership in grace.
